item.title: NAVADMIN 114/26 - NAVY RESERVE PROMOTIONS TO THE PERMANENT GRADES OF CAPTAIN, COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT, AND CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER IN THE LINE AND STAFF CORPS
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

Date: Not specified in the document

Subject: Notification of Permanent Promotions for Selected Naval Personnel

To: All Concerned

This communication serves to announce permanent promotions for a distinguis
distinguished group of naval personnel. These advancements are effective fr
from various dates starting from December 2024 and running through June 202
2026.

**Details of Promotions:**

1. **Promotion to Lieutenant Commander (Commander Grade):**
- Several officers have been promoted to the rank of Lieutenant Commande
Commander, with specific details pertaining to their respective grade advan
advancements as Commanders effective from different dates in December 2024
and beyond.

2. **Promotion to Lieutenant:**
- A significant number of junior officers will be promoted to the rank o
of Lieutenant:
- Effective Dec 1, 2024
- Effective Jan 1, 2025
- Effective Mar 1, 2025
- Effective June 1, 2026

3. **Promotion to Ensign:**
- Several naval officers have been promoted to the rank of Ensign with d
detailed effective dates listed as follows:
- Dec 1, 2024
- Jan 1, 2025
- Mar 1, 2025

**Conclusion:**

This document outlines the promotions granted to various naval officers fro
from different grade levels across a range of effective dates. The specific
specific names and ranks are listed in detail for reference.

For any further inquiries or clarifications related to these promotions, pl
please contact the designated Naval Personnel Office.

---

Please note that this is a summary based on the provided information; detai
detailed lists of names and exact effective dates are contained within the
document.

item.title: NAVADMIN 115/26 - NAVY WOUNDED WARRIOR ANNOUNCES THE TEAM NAVY ROSTER FOR 2026 WARRIOR GAMES

The U.S. Navy has announced the Team Navy roster for the 2026 Warrior Games

Guardsmen.

Who is affected:
Sailors and Coast Guardsmen

Important dates:
June 2026

Required action:
None stated

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 113/26 - ACTIVE-DUTY PROMOTIONS TO THE PERMANENT GRADES OF CAPTAIN, COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT, AND CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER IN THE LINE AND STAFF CORPS
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

Who: Various Navy personnel receiving promotions

What: Promotions to different ranks in the Navy, including Captain (Captain
(Captain), Lieutenant Commander (Commander), Lieutenant (Lieutenant), and E
Ensign (Ensign).

When: Different dates for various promotions ranging from July 2024 to June
June 2026.

Where: Not specified in the text.

Why: Recognition of achievements and advancements in military career paths.
paths.

Key Dates:
- DOR/EFF DATE 12 JULY 2024
- DOR/EFF DATE 01 FEBRUARY 2025
- DOR/EFF DATE 01 APRIL 2025
- DOR/EFF DATE 01 JUNE 2026

This notice lists numerous individuals receiving promotions to various rank
ranks within the Navy, with specific effective dates provided for each rank
rank advancement.

item.title: NAVADMIN 110/26 - SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY PROGRAM ANNUAL RECERTIFICATION
item.description:

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has opened the annual recertification window for Special Duty
Duty Assignment Pay (SDAP) and commands must complete the process by Septem
September 30, 2026, to avoid payment disruptions.

Who is affected:
Commands responsible for SDAP

Important dates:
Recertification window closes on 30 September 2026

Required action:
Review updated instructions and complete recertification process by deadlin
deadline

Verify details against the full official message.

item.description:

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has announced the implementation of spot promotions for Lieut
Lieutenant billets within the Naval nuclear propulsion program, aimed at re
recognizing and compensating officers with critical skills and responsibili
responsibilities. The initiative includes eligibility criteria, designated
billets, and efforts to engage fleet commands and mentor junior Sailors.

Who is affected:
Nuclear LDOs in specific billets

Important dates:
1 October 2026

Required action:
None stated

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 107/26 - C-NOte #8: CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT AS WARFIGHTING ADVANTAGE
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is emphasizing continuous learning and improvement as a strat
strategic warfighting advantage, focusing on the implementation of the Tier
Tiered Learning System (TLS) to ensure rapid dissemination and application
of lessons learned across all levels.

Who is affected:
All members of the U.S. Navy

Important dates:
None stated

Required action:
Not specified

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 106/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 RESERVE OFFICER RETENTION AND CONTINUATION BOARDS
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is announcing the FY-27 Reserve Officer Retention and Continu
Continuation boards for Selected Reserve, Individual Ready Reserve, and Sta
Standby Reserve-Active officers. The boards will convene on January 18, 202
2027, and July 19, 2027, to review applications for eligibility based on pa
paygrade, age, designator limitations, and attrition dates.

Who is affected:
Reserve Officers in the Selected Reserve, Individual Ready Reserve, and Sta
Standby Reserve-Active (USNR-S1).

Important dates:
January 18, 2027; July 19, 2027

Required action:
Submit applications by November 15, 2026, for January board or May 15, 2027
2027, for July board.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 104/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 PERMANENT MILITARY INSTRUCTOR SELECTION BOARD
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy announces the FY27 Permanent Military Instructor selection bo
board for officers serving at the US Naval Academy in July 2027, with a max
maximum of 8 positions available to LCDRs released by their current communi
communities.

Who is affected:
Officers eligible for or serving as Lieutenant Commander (O4) and reporting
reporting to the USNA in July 2027.

Important dates:
Applications due: October 1, 2026

Required action:
Submit applications via DoD SAFE or mail them to Navy Personnel Command by
the deadline.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 105/26 - EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY FOR SIMULATED HURRICANE PILIPO AFTER STORM PASSAGE (HURREX 26)
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has issued an order to account for all personnel within desig
designated commands in Hawaii after a simulated hurricane passage. Commands
Commands must ensure their databases are accurate and conduct muster operat
operations following the storm.

Who is affected:
All Navy active duty, reserve, DON civilian employees, and applicable depen
dependents within designated Hawaii commands.

Important dates:
None stated.

Required action:
Commanding officers and sponsors must ensure accurate personnel accountabil
accountability via NFAAS or through direct chain of command contact if NFAA
NFAAS is unavailable.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 103/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY RESERVE NATIONAL COMMAND AND SENIOR OFFICER APPLY BOARD
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

The Navy Reserve National Command and Senior Officer APPLY Board for Fiscal
Fiscal Year 2027 is scheduled to convene on August 10, 2026, to assign comm
command and leadership positions. Eligible officers must submit their appli
applications through the RFMT website by July 30, 2026.

Who is affected:
Navy Reserve Selected Reserve (SELRES) officers

Important dates:
Application deadline: July 30, 2026; Fitness Report deadline: August 4, 202
2026

Required action:
Submit applications and fitness reports by the specified deadlines

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 102/26 - HEALTH OF THE FORCE SURVEY
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

The Health of the Force Survey for Active Duty and Reserve Component Sailor
Sailors opens on May 15, 2026, and closes on June 30, 2026. The survey is v
voluntary and confidential, aiming to gather data that will help address ke
key personnel issues affecting warfighter effectiveness.

Who is affected:
Active Duty and Reserve Component Sailors

Important dates:
May 15 - June 30, 2026

Required action:
Participate in the Health of the Force Survey

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 100/26 - C-NOte #7: GOLDEN FLEET INITIATIVE AND OUR FUTURE FLEET DESIGN
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy's Golden Fleet Initiative (GFI) aims to enhance maritime domi
dominance and combat capability by accelerating ship construction and refor
reforming industrial and business practices. The initiative includes fundin
funding for 34 new ships in the FY27 budget, emphasizing a high-low mix of
forces for both high-end combat and daily operations.

Who is affected:
U.S. Navy personnel

Important dates:
None stated

Required action:
None stated

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 101/26 - NAVY UNIFORM UPDATES TO NAVY WORKING UNIFORM AND SERVICE DRESS BLUES
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is updating uniform regulations for Service Dress Blue (SDB)
and authorizing the wear of NWU Type III in the Pentagon starting May 4, 20
2026. The changes aim to enhance flexibility and align with joint force sta
standards.

Who is affected:
All Sailors

Important dates:
May 4, 2026

Required action:
Adhere to updated uniform regulations as specified.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 099/26 - FY26 PROJECTED SHIP INACTIVATION SCHEDULE (UPDATE #2)
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has updated the FY26 projected ship inactivation schedule, pr
providing new inactive dates for several ships including USS NEWPORT NEWS,
USS ALEXANDRIA, and others. Fleet Commanders are required to submit Organiz
Organizational Change Requests for decommissioned or inactivated ships.

Who is affected:
Fleet Commanders and relevant Naval commands

Important dates:
None stated

Required action:
Submit Organizational Change Requests as per OPNAVINST 5400.44B

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 098/26 - IMPLEMENTING POLICY ON PRIORITIZING MILITARY EXCELLENCE AND READINESS - INVOLUNTARY SEPARATIONS
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is implementing policy on prioritizing military excellence an
and readiness by initiating involuntary separations for Sailors diagnosed w
with gender dysphoria. The Commander, Navy Installations Command will act a
as the consolidated disposition authority.

Who is affected:
Active duty, Reserve, and Individual Ready Reserve Sailors.

Important dates:
None stated.

Required action:
Commands must identify and refer Service Members for separation processing
within 5 working days if they meet criteria outlined in reference (a).

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 097/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2026 FLIGHT HOUR VERIFICATION REQUIREMENT FOR CONDITIONAL AVIATION INCENTIVE PAY AND HAZARDOUS DUTY INCENTIVE PAY FOR FLYING
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is implementing a fiscal year 2026 flight hour verification p
process for conditional Aviation Incentive Pay (AvIP) and Hazardous Duty In
Incentive Pay for Flying (HDIP-F). Commanding officers are required to veri
verify the accuracy of reported flight hours by endorsing verification lett
letters on command letterhead.

Who is affected:
Officers receiving conditional AvIP or HDIP-F

Important dates:
30 November 2026, 31 December 2026

Required action:
Report total flight hours from October 2025 through September 2026 to PERS-
PERS-435A by specified deadlines.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 096/26 - MRS. SYBIL STOCKDALE OMBUDSMAN OF THE YEAR AWARD
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
NAVADMIN announces the nomination and selection process for the annual Mrs.
Mrs. Sybil Stockdale Ombudsman of the Year Award for Calendar Year 2026. El
Eligible Navy Ombudsmen who have served in good standing for at least one y
year are invited to apply by May 29, 2026.

Who is affected:
Navy Ombudsmen

Important dates:
May 29, 2026; June 19, 2026; June 26, 2026

Required action:
Submit nomination packages by May 29, 2026

Verify details against the full official message.

(IDC) of the Year awardees across various categories, recognizing their out
outstanding performance and dedication to health care.

Independent Duty Corpsmen

Important dates:

Required action:

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 094/26 - UPDATED GUIDANCE ON DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY CIVILIAN TRAVEL
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has issued updated policy guidance for civilian employee trav
travel, rescinding previous guidance and restoring government travel card l
limits. Essential travel categories are outlined and approval processes for
for both essential and non-essential travel are clarified.

Who is affected:
U.S. Navy civilian employees

Important dates:
Effective immediately

Required action:
Civilian supervisors must approve essential travel; Flag Officer or Senior
Executive Service Member approves non-essential travel

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 093/26 - 2026 COMMUNICATIONS SECURITY (COMSEC) MONITORING NOTIFICATION
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is notifying all units about the ongoing Communications Secur
Security (COMSEC) monitoring in place to ensure protection of telecommunica
telecommunications and IT systems from security threats. Commanding Officer
Officers must regularly inform their personnel that these systems are subje
subject to COMSEC monitoring.

Who is affected:
All Navy personnel using official Department of War telecommunication and I
IT systems.

Important dates:
1 July 2026

Required action:
Fleet maritime operations centers, Naval component commanders, and Echelon
2 commanders must verify implementation by subordinate commands and provide
provide notice to U.S. Fleet Cyber Command by the stated date.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 092/26 - ORDER TO UPDATE NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM PERSONAL AND EMERGENCY CONTACT INFORMATION (APRIL 2026)
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has issued a directive for all uniformed and civilian personn
personnel, including contractors outside the continental United States, to
update their personal and emergency contact information in the Navy Family
Accountability and Assessment System (NFAAS) by May 15, 2026.

Who is affected:
All Navy uniformed and civilian personnel, including OCONUS contractors

Important dates:
May 15, 2026

Required action:
Update personal and emergency contact information in NFAAS

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 091/26 - EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY AFTER STORM PASSAGE/CLOSEST POINT OF APPROACH (CPA) IN CONJUNCTION WITH HURREX-CITADEL GALE 26 (HURRICANE BRAVO-TX)
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has issued an order to account for its personnel and dependen
dependents after the passage of Hurricane Bravo in specified counties withi
within Texas. Commands must ensure accurate data in the NFAAS database and
muster all affected individuals.

Who is affected:
Navy active duty, reserve members, civilian employees, contractors, and app
applicable dependents in designated areas.

Important dates:
None stated

Required action:
Ensure NFAAS databases are up-to-date and muster personnel after storm pass
passage.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 090/26 - EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM IN CONJUNCTION WITH HURRICANE ALPHA 2026
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy is initiating the Family Accountability and Assessment System
System in response to Hurricane Alpha 2026 for personnel in specified geogr
geographic areas of North Carolina and Virginia.

Who is affected:
Navy active duty, reserves, civilian employees, NAF/NEX employees, and thei
their dependents within designated coastal land areas of North Carolina and
and Virginia.

Important dates:
None stated

Required action:
Ensure the Navy Family Accountability and Assessment System (NFAAS) databas
database is updated with current contact information for all personnel in t
the affected geographic area. Verify accountability after the storm passes
or when ordered to muster.

Verify details against the full official message.

item.title: NAVADMIN 086/27 - ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY TYPHOON SINLAKU JOINT REGION MARIANAS GUAM
item.description:
# AI Summary

_AI-generated summary. Verify details against the full official message._

AI Summary:
The U.S. Navy has ordered all personnel within the Joint Region Marianas to
to account for themselves and their dependents in response to Typhoon Sinla
Sinlakou, ensuring accurate information is recorded in the Navy Family Acco
Accountability and Assessment System (NFAAS).

Who is affected:
All Navy active duty, reserve members, civilian employees, contractors, and
and applicable dependents in Guam, Rota, Tinian, and Saipan.

Important dates:
None stated.

Required action:
Ensure accurate personnel accountability data in NFAAS or contact designate
designated points of contact as specified.

Verify details against the full official message.
